message:
perf(vdbe): promote IsNull into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Sixth opcode-promotion lever on bd-1dp9.6.2, following AddImm
(64d8ce4d), Copy (20b05a95), SCopy (bd1a49bc), DecrJumpZero
(7b7bd71c), and IfPos (27da4f32). `Opcode::IsNull` is the
highest-frequency unpromoted opcode in codegen — 87 emit sites,
more than Goto/Column/Copy/Null individually. It backs every
NOT NULL constraint check, every IS NULL / IS NOT NULL WHERE
clause, and the null-guarding boilerplate around aggregate
folding, CASE/COALESCE evaluation, and JOIN match probing. The
body is the smallest possible shape — a single `is_null` read +
branch — so dispatch routing cost dominates work cost, which is
exactly the workload where hot-path pre-filter pays off.

Mirrors the existing main-match arm verbatim. Adds a
`test_is_null_via_hot_path` unit test pinning both branches
(NULL → jump-taken / non-NULL → fall-through) to the hot-path
arm by interleaving NULL and Integer source registers. Adds a
`vdbe_pipeline_execute_isnull` bench arm to pipeline_stages.rs
mirroring the always-taken-jump shape used for IfPos (each op's
p2 jump target is the immediately-next instruction, so the body
runs the real branch but execution sequencing stays linear).

Win sizes (criterion, p<0.05, /256 signal-cleanest size):

| variant | before | after | Δ thrpt |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| /64   | 504.84 ns | 422.09 ns | +19.6% |
| /256  | 1.7299 µs | 1.3571 µs | +27.5% |
| /1024 | 6.6484 µs | 5.2236 µs | +27.2% |

Larger than the ~5% baseline projected from DecrJumpZero — same
surprise pattern as IfPos (+19.6% at /256). The IsNull main-match
arm sat in a cold position in the 190-arm match, so promoting it
shortens the per-op instruction window meaningfully on top of the
expected jump-table-slot savings.

Verified via:
- 4 hot-path unit tests pass (is_null + decr_jump_zero + if_pos +
  scopy via_hot_path), confirming the new arm is observed exactly
  the same way as its sibling promotions.
- Stash/pop comparison on the full fsqlite-vdbe lib test suite:
  baseline 732 pass / 45 fail (pre-existing concurrent-agent
  state); with this change 734 pass / 44 fail. Net: +2 tests
  passing (incl. the new is_null), 0 regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
